lesson.title: Cointegration & Pairs Trading
lesson.body: Pairs trading identifies two cointegrated stocks where the spread y_t − β·x_t is stationary. The hedge ratio β is estimated by OLS regression. The spread is standardized into a z-score: z_t = (spread_t − mean)/std. Typical entry: |z| > 2; exit: |z| < 0.5. Long the cheaper stock, short the expensive one when the spread diverges.

rooms.question: In pairs trading with stocks A and B, when the spread is 2 standard deviations above its mean, you should:
answers: Short A and long B (the spread should narrow)
rooms.explanation: Spread = A − β·B. Spread too high means A is expensive relative to B → short A, long B. You profit when the spread narrows.

rooms.question: The hedge ratio β in pairs trading is estimated by:
answers: OLS regression of stock A on stock B
rooms.explanation: OLS regression gives β that minimizes variance of the spread. Note: in practice, many use total least squares (TLS) or Kalman filter for a dynamic β.

rooms.question: The z-score for entry in pairs trading is computed as:
rooms.explanation: Z-score normalizes the spread by subtracting its mean and dividing by its standard deviation, making it dimensionless and comparable to historical norms.

rooms.question: Order the pairs trading strategy implementation steps:
items: Estimate hedge ratio β by OLS regression
Enter when |z| > 2, exit when |z| < 0.5, stop-loss at |z| > 3
rooms.explanation: Pairs trading pipeline: find cointegrated pairs → estimate β → build z-score signal → trade the signal with clear entry/exit rules.

rooms.question: Pairs trading is considered market-neutral because:
answers: Long and short positions offset broad market moves (beta exposure ≈ 0)
Both stocks are in the same sector
You hold equal dollar amounts in each stock
The strategy uses only limit orders
rooms.explanation: With hedge ratio β, long one stock and short β units of the other approximately neutralizes market beta exposure — the strategy profits from relative moves, not direction.

boss.name: The Diverging Pair
boss.taunt: Your spread never closes. Your cointegration was just correlation in disguise.

questions.question: A key risk in pairs trading is 'regime change'. This means:
answers: The cointegrating relationship breaks down, and the spread keeps diverging
Both stocks become correlated with the market
questions.explanation: Cointegrating relationships can and do break down (mergers, sector rotations, fundamental changes). The trade must have a stop-loss for when the spread diverges beyond expectations.

questions.question: Using a static (OLS-estimated) hedge ratio in pairs trading can fail because:
answers: The true hedge ratio changes over time (non-stationarity of β)
OLS is not the correct regression technique
The regression must be run on returns, not prices
questions.explanation: A time-varying β (from Kalman filter or rolling OLS) better adapts to changing market conditions. Static β from a 1-year regression may be stale.

questions.question: The Johansen test is preferred over Engle-Granger for pairs trading when:
answers: Testing multiple pairs simultaneously (more than 2 assets)
The hedge ratio is known in advance
questions.explanation: Johansen naturally extends to k>2 assets (portfolio/basket trading) and correctly identifies all cointegrating vectors, avoiding the single-equation bias in Engle-Granger.
